mnemonics: Greta Makes Ljung-Box Tests: Think 'Greta Makes Ljung' to remember she co-created the Ljung-Box test for checking autocorrelation in time series residuals.
keyPoints: Co-creator of the Ljung-Box Q-test, a statistical test used to determine if autocorrelations of a time series significantly differ from zero.
The test is applied to the residuals of a model to check for remaining serial correlation, which indicates a poor model fit.
Her work is fundamental in model diagnostics and validation within time series analysis, particularly for ARIMA models.
commonMistakes: Applying the Ljung-Box test to the original time series data instead of the model's residuals.
Misinterpreting a high p-value as a sign of a bad model; a high p-value (e.g., >0.05) actually indicates no significant autocorrelation and is generally a good result for residuals.
Using an inappropriate number of lags (h) in the test, which can lead to incorrect conclusions about the presence of autocorrelation.
